Baphia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. Baphia species, such as Baphia nitida and Baphia afromontana, have been traditionally used in various cultures for their medicinal properties. The health benefits of Baphia species include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Baphia species include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Baphia species include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
